text:
There once was a wise old owl named Old Wilbur. He lived high in a towering oak tree, far away from the prying eyes of humans.

One autumn evening, a young squirrel named fluffy came bouncing down the treebranch toward Old Wilbur.

“I’m curious, Old Wilbur,” fuzzy said. “What sorts of adventures have you had across the forests?”

With a wise old chuckle, Wilbur recounted tales of mysterious moonlit glades, battles against jabbing thorns, and daring foraging expeditions into abandoned cabins.

There was one particularly fascinating story Wilbur shared. During a particularly cold winter, a great flock of nocturnal birds had hatched young. They had built a gigantic nest full of seeds and sang sonnets at midnight.

“My dear fuzzy,” Wilbur smiled. “It’s a secret garden where the young stars learn to sing.”

Fluffy’s eyes sparkled with wonder. “Oh, I wish I could visit.”

“Then,” said Wilbur,
